top_signals.title: Japan’s July oil imports to return to pre-Iran war level, Takaichi says
top_signals.summary: Japan relied on the Middle East for over 90% of its oil imports but has significantly diversified its supply chain.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Rise, Oil Falls as Trump Halts Iran Strikes: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Wall Street traders drove stocks higher as oil fell after President Donald Trump said he canceled scheduled strikes against Iran, fueling hopes for a diplomatic way out of the war that has rattled global markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Kevin Warsh takes oath of office as chairman and a member of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, and the Federal Open Market Committee unanimously selects Warsh as its chairman

top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Qatari-donated Air Force One now sports red, white and blue paint job
top_signals.summary: The interim Air Force One VC-25B Bridge aircraft is undergoing its final modifications ahead of a summer rollout.

top_signals.title: Centerview, Venezuela Discussed $150 Million Fee for Debt Rework
top_signals.summary: Centerview Partners was in negotiations with Venezuela as recently as last month for a contract worth at least $150 million for its work as the sole financial adviser on the country’s debt restructuring, a sum that would far exceed payouts for past sovereign deals, according to a draft of the agreement seen by Bloomberg.

top_signals.summary: Fallout from knife attack and riots accelerated by online posting, current and former officers say
